name: Brisket and Beans/Crockpot or Pressure Cooker
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the meat and place it in a large pot. Cover with about 2 cups of water.
Vegetables can be added to the pot along with seasonings for cooking in a slow cooker for 10 hours.
2. In a Dutch oven, simmer the brisket for 2 to 2 1/2 hours before adding vegetables,
then continue until the potatoes are done.
3. In a pressure cooker, bring the brisket, water, and seasonings to 15 psi pressure
and cook for 30 to 40 minutes. Reduce the pressure under cold water, open the cooker,
add vegetables, cover, bring to 15 psi pressure again, and cook for 5 minutes.
Then reduce the pressure once more. Remove the meat, slice it thinly on the diagonal,
and serve with green beans and potatoes drenched in cooking liquid. Do not thicken
the natural gravy. It should have a lovely flavor and be non-greasy.
If the meat is not well trimmed, remove excess fat before cooking.
Leftovers can be reheated using the remaining liquid and sliced for open-faced or French dip sandwiches.
